last_run_window: Wed 22 Jul 2026, afternoon / 22 Jul 14:00 UTC (delta window: 5 NEW stories since 20:00 UTC 21 Jul — Iran escalation day 10, Meta $115-135B AI capex confirmed, Treasury yields spike 4.63%, EU DMA forces Android/AI opening, Brazil tariff effective)

stories.headline: Google Alphabet Postpones Gemini 3.5 Pro Release by Months; Model Shows Shortfalls in Coding Performance and Long-Horizon Reasoning vs Competitive Set; Alphabet Shares Fall 4%; Signals AI Model Race Tightening and ROI Questions on Infrastructure Capex Durability

TSMC and Samsung Raise Advanced-Node Wafer Prices 5–10% Effective July 2026: 5nm/7nm Nodes (74% of TSMC Foundry Revenue); Smartphone Chips +5%, CPUs +7%, HPC/AI Chips +10%; Reflects Pricing Power Amid AI Demand But Creates Input-Cost Pressure for Downstream Customers (NVDA, AMD, Apple, Qualcomm)

June 2026 CPI Beats Consensus: Headline Falls to 3.5% YoY (vs 3.8% consensus), Core Flats to 2.6% YoY (vs 2.9% forecast); Headline -0.4% MoM Shock Drop (vs -0.2% consensus); Energy Index -5.7% MoM Largest Drop Since April 2020; Disinflation Thesis Confirmed, Rate-Cut Odds Rise Sharply

Treasury Yields Sink on CPI Relief: 10-Year Falls 6bp to 4.57%, 2-Year Drops 14bp to 4.14% on July 15–16; Curve Steepens Inversion (10y/2y Spread Rises to +43bp); Market Reprices September Rate Hike Odds to ~58% (from 67%); Bullish for Long-Duration Tech Equities

ASML Raises 2026 Full-Year Revenue Guidance to €43-45B (from €36-40B) with 54-56% Gross Margin (from 51-53%); Q2 Beat €9.3B Revenue (vs €8.8B consensus); CEO Fouquet: 'Demand Outpacing Supply'; EUV Shipments Target +25% YoY; Capex Durability Thesis Confirmed

Samsung Electronics Q2 2026 Earnings Miss Expectations Despite Record Revenue; Memory/Chip Guidance Signals Lower 2H 2026 Demand Growth; Triggers Global Semiconductor Sector Selloff (Nasdaq-100 –1.8%, Intel –9.7%, AMD –6.5%, ASML –7.3%)

Iran Escalation Deepens: Day 10 Conflict with $37.5B Estimated Cost; Ceasefire Collapsed July 8; IRGC Strikes U.S. Bases Kuwait/Bahrain; Iran Attacks Strait Shipping; US Launches SECOND WAVE Strikes July 15 Degrading Iranian Ability to Target Strait Vessels; Blockade Reimposed Both Sides; Oil Surges to $88.10 Brent, $82.49+ WTI (12% Weekly Gain); IEA: 'Largest Supply Disruption in History'; Manila Diplomatic Talks July 21–22 Signal Crisis Persistence; Further Escalation Risk Remains High; Strait of Hormuz Disruption Risk Materializing; Stagflation Pressure on Tech Sector

Iran Escalation Cascades to Semiconductor Supply Chain: Iran Attacks Qatar LNG Facilities, Damages One Production Train (3.8 MTPA capacity); Helium Output Falls 14% (Global Supply Cut ~5%); Minimum One-Year Recovery Timeline; Critical for EUV Lithography Tool Heat Management; Supply Shock Emerges Despite Capex Durability

Fed Minutes Release July 8, 2:00 PM ET: Nine of 18 Officials Project ≥1 Rate Hike in Remainder 2026; Fed Chair Warsh Omits Own Projection from June Dot Plot, Signals Data Dependency; CPI Beat July 14 Offsets Hawkish Surprise; Rate-Cut Pricing Accelerates Post-CPI

US Data-Center Power Demand Tops 1,100 TWh Annually; Grid Interconnection Queue Delays Remain 24–36 Months; ESCALATION: ~50% of Planned 2026 Data Centers Canceled/Delayed Due to Power Constraints; PARTIAL RELIEF: National Grid Ventures Invests $1.75B for 35% Stake in Joulent; Chevron-Microsoft Project Kilby Adds 2.67 GW Dedicated Behind-Meter Capacity (2028); Grid Constraint Remains Binding Near-Term But Addressable

Section 301 China Tariff Hearing Concludes July 7; 12.5% Rate on Chinese Goods Finalized July 15; Semiconductor Tariff Rate Remains 0% (Effective Dec 23, 2025) with Escalation Scheduled June 23, 2027; No Near-Term Acceleration; Raises Semiconductor Input Costs and AI Chip Materials Prices

China Halts Helium Exports Effective July 10, 2026; Compounds Iran-Qatar Supply Disruption; China Imports 80%+ of Global Helium; Creates Asymmetric Supply Pressure on Chipmakers Sourcing from China; Supply Chain Fragility Exposed
Goldman Sachs Downgrades Semiconductor Sector; Cuts SMH Price Target 5%, Cites 2027 Capex Intensity and 3–5% Earnings Revisions Down; Earnings Reset Now Reflected in Samsung/Global Chip Selloff

Semiconductor Market Reverses Post-TSMC Capex Guidance: PHLX Semiconductor Index (SOX) Down 10% for Week; Taiwan Semiconductor Equities Enter Correction Territory (Down 25% from June Peak); Market Reinterprets TSMC Capex Raise ($60–64B) as Oversupply Signal Rather Than Demand Validation; Concerns: AI Capex Efficiency Questions, Supply-Demand Imbalance, Demand Slowdown Risk

TSMC Q2 2026 Earnings CRUSH + GUIDANCE UPREVISION: Revenue $40.04B (+36% YoY), Gross Margin 67.7% (beat 65.5–67.5%); FY2026 Revenue Growth Raised to >40% (from >30%); Capex Increased to $60–64B (from $56B ceiling); Q3 Guidance $44.6–45.8B; AI Demand Acceleration Outpacing Prior Expectations; CoWoS Sold Out; Validates Chipmaker Capex Durability [NOTE: Market reinterpretation post-July 19 shows sell-off; capex durability thesis questioned]

June NFP +57K (Miss vs. 110K Consensus); Downward Revisions to May/April Payrolls; Unemployment Falls to 4.2% but Driven by Labor Force Attrition; Insured Duration Rises to 25.5 Weeks (vs 21.5 YoY); September Rate-Hike Odds Drop to ~58% (from 67%) Despite Warsh 'Inflation Too High' Stance

Intel Foundry Services Backlog Grows to $15B+ (from $8B Six Months Prior); Apple Partnership Validates Foundry-as-Service Model; Idled Fab Capacity Filling; VALIDATION: Intel First to Ship High-NA EUV Logic Production; 18A Node Yield Improving; Partial Offset to Samsung Capex Doubt

Micron Technology Announces Up to $3 Billion Strategic Investment in US Semiconductor Ecosystem; $500M to GlobalWafers for 300mm Raw Silicon Wafer Manufacturing; 10-Year Supply Agreement; Supports AI Capacity Buildout; Signals Capex Durability Conviction

AI Capital Spend Uprevised to $750B (2026) → $1T+ (2027); Power Grid Becomes Binding Constraint (Not GPU Supply); AWS Launches Graviton5 CPU Instances; Qualcomm-Meta AI Data-Center Partnership; GPU Market Projected $624.17B by 2034; Hyperscaler Demand 'Almost Unlimited'; Infrastructure Diversification Away from NVIDIA Pure-Play
Section 122 Global Tariff (10%) Expires July 24; Congress Unlikely to Renew Before July 25 Recess; Creates August Tariff Uncertainty and Capex Limbo (Samsung Delays $10B US Fab Decision)

US State Department Signals Potential TSMC Export Rule Relaxation if Company Commits to US-Based N-1 (Leading-Edge) Fabrication by 2028; First Explicit Carrot in Negotiated Capacity Buildout; De-Escalation Signal on Export Controls

NVIDIA H200 Yield Issues Emerging at Scale; Advanced Node Design Tool Limitations Approaching; Impacts Near-Term GPU Supply and Pricing Power; Technical Constraints on Production Ramp vs. Demand Growth
SK Hynix Begins Trading on Nasdaq July 10, 2026; Largest Foreign IPO to Date; Successful Debut Validates Memory Chip Demand Recovery Narrative and Eases Supply Constraints on DRAM/NAND

China Mulls AI Export Controls; Ministry of Commerce Holds Talks with Alibaba, ByteDance on Restricting Overseas Access to Advanced AI Models; Tiered System Proposed (Basic Open-Source Filing → Advanced Security Review → Frontier Models Barred); Creates Asymmetric Advantage for US AI Ecosystem

Chinese AI Startup Moonshot AI Releases Advanced Model to Rival US AI Leadership; Investors Fear Technology Could Undercut US AI Firm Revenue and Market Share; Signals China's AI-Sector Acceleration Narrowing US Competitive Moat; Raises Long-Term Competition Risk for US AI Infrastructure Capex ROI

Netflix Q2 2026 Earnings MISS + Market Reaction: Revenue Guidance $50.7B–$51.7B FY2026 at 31.5% Op Margin; Advertising ~$3B in 2026; Subscriber/Revenue Growth Weaker Than Expected; Stock Declined 10% Post-Earnings; Reporting Change: Shifts 'What We Watched' from Quarterly to Annual Format (Engagement Opacity Concerns); Sentiment Reversal From Positive Guidance Narrative

Analyst Downgrades Escalate: KeyBanc Downgrades AAPL to Underweight ($250 PT); SFDC to Sector Weight; Baird Downgrades CoStar (SaaS) $48→$34 PT; Raymond James Downgrades Check Point; Multiple Valuations Compressed Post-Earnings
Brazil 25% Section 301 Tariff Imposed Effective July 22, 2026; Joins 60-Country Forced-Labor Tariff Pipeline (10–12.5%); Semiconductor Input Costs Rise (Materials, Packaging, Rare Earths)

Microsoft Q2 2026 Earnings Beat BUT Cloud Growth Guidance Caution Shifts Market Narrative: EPS $4.14 (beat $3.86), Revenue $81.27B (beat); BUT Azure Growth Guidance Cautious; Stock Down ~9% Post-Earnings; Market Rerates from AI-Durability Bullish to Capex-Normalization Bearish; Signals Enterprise Capex May Normalize Away from Broad Cloud to Targeted AI-Only Spend

Samsung Electronics Reaffirms $73B Semiconductor CapEx 2026; Memory + Foundry + R&D Expansion; Taylor Texas Fab Targets Operational Readiness End 2026, Risk Production H2 2026; Reversal of Earnings-Miss Capex Doubt; Signals Foundry-as-Service Model Durability; Validates TSMC Concentration Risk Mitigation

Intel CapEx Guidance Flat-to-Down in 2026 After $17.7B 2025 Spend (–29% YoY Decline); Foundry Services Restructuring Signals Execution Headwinds; Contrasts Sharply with TSMC +$60–64B and Samsung +$73B; Market Interprets as Admission Foundry Model Cannot Scale Competitively; Supply Concentration on TSMC/Samsung Tightens

Intel Shares Surge to Record Highs (Up 7%+) on Speculation Apple Mulling Intel and Samsung for Advanced Chip Manufacturing; Marks Reversal of Bearish Capex Pullback Narrative; TSMC Stock Down 2% on Sourcing Diversification Concerns; Validates Foundry-as-Service Model Viability and Reduces TSMC Concentration Risk

NVIDIA Discloses 9.3% Passive Stake in Nebius; Market Interprets as Strategic Interest in AI Infrastructure Diversification Away from NVIDIA GPU Pure-Play Model; Signals Hyperscaler Preference for Custom-Silicon and Non-GPU AI Accelerator Diversification; Validates Power-Constrained Capex Scenarios

AI-Linked Equities Reach $27 Trillion Market Value, Representing 36% of S&P 500; Bubble Concentration Intensifies Amid Elevated Credit Costs and Leverage in AI Acquisition; Revaluation Risk if AI Capex ROI Disappoints or Demand Growth Slows

Meta AI Capex Surge: $115–135B in 2026 (+59–87% YoY vs. $72B in 2025) Confirms Multi-Year AI Investment Cycle; Q2 Beat Signals Confidence in AI ROI Despite Chip-Sector Capex Doubt; Directly Contradicts Semiconductor Sector Capex Efficiency Questions

US 10-Year Treasury Yields Surge to 4.63% on Inflation Tail-Risk from Iran Escalation; Highest Level Since Mid-May 2026; Equity Discount-Rate Pressure Mounts; Second Consecutive Day of Gains; Bearish for Long-Duration Tech Valuations

EU Digital Markets Act: Google Forced to Open Android to AI Competitors & Share Search Data; Binding Order Erodes Platform Defensibility; Search Data Sharing Begins Jan 2027, Android Interoperability Required Jul 2027; Regulatory Tightening on AI Distribution Channels

Brazil Section 301 Tariff: 25% Effective July 22, 2026; Targets Big Tech Platforms (X, Meta, Google Content Removal Orders); Semiconductor Input Costs Rise (Materials, Packaging); Signals Section 301 Escalation Continuing; Modest Impact on US Mega-Cap Brazil Exposure

White House Frontier AI Model Review: Federal 30-Day Review Gate Before Frontier Model Release Coming; Regulatory Framework Clarifies But Adds Deployment Friction for Model Companies; Net-Neutral for Mega-Cap Tech (Resources to Comply), Mildly Negative for AI Startups
